zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Mowbray於1243年出生在英國林可爾史爾時，她的父親Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk24歲，母親Unknown de Furnival22歲。她於1261年與Robert de Mohaut在英國威爾斯福林爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1284年1月11日之後在英國英格蘭諾福爾可卡素 利辛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Mowbray于1243年出生在英国林可爾史爾时，她的父亲Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk24岁，母亲Unknown de Furnival22岁。她于1261年与Robert de Mohaut在英国威尔斯福林爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1284年1月11日之后在英国英格兰諾福爾可卡素 利辛。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mowbray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from Montbray (Manche). Robert de Mowbray was Earl of Northumbria 1086–95, but was later imprisoned and deposed for rebelling against William II.

variants.name: Mulberry
meanings: English (Middlesex and Cambridgeshire): variant of   Mowbray  .
Irish (Derry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maoilbhearaigh ‘descendant of the devotee of (Saint) Bearach’.

variants.name: Mayberry
meanings: English: most probably an altered form of   Mowbray  . It is also found as   Maybury  , which has the form of an English habitational name. There is a place near Woking in Surrey so called; however, this is not recorded until 1885 and is probably derived from the surname. In England this surname is found mainly in the West Midlands; it has also spread into Wales. In Ireland this form is common in Ulster; MacLysaght records that it was taken there from England in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Mowbray was born in 1243, in Lincolnshire, England, her father, Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk, was 24 and her mother, Unknown de Furnival, was 22. She married Robert de Mohaut in 1261, in Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died after 11 January 1284, in Castle Rising, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Mowbray est née en 1243, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Unknown de Furnival, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Robert de Mohaut en 1261, à Flintshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 11 janvier 1284, à Castle Rising, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Mowbray è nata il nel 1243 nel Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Unknown de Furnival, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Robert de Mohaut nel 1261, nel Flintshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 11 gennaio 1284, nel Castle Rising, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Mowbrayが1243年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirskは24歳，母Unknown de Furnivalは22歳であった。1261年にイギリスウェールズフリントシュアイアでRobert de Mohautと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1284年1月11日以後にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクカストル リシングで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Mowbray(이)가 1243년 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Unknown de Furnival(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1261년 영국 웨일스 플린츠히르에서 Robert de Mohaut(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1284년 1월 11일 이후 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 카스틀 리징에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Mowbray nasceu em 1243, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Unknown de Furnival, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert de Mohaut em 1261, em Flintshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 11 de janeiro de 1284, em Castle Rising, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Mowbray родилась в 1243 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger Mowbray Baron of Thirsk было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Unknown de Furnival, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Mohaut в 1261 г. в местности флинцхир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 11 январь 1284 г. в местности кастл рисинг, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.
